title: Tomato Gojju
description: A thick, spicy, tangy tomato gojju with crunchy onions, crushed garlic, fresh coriander, and tamarind. Perfect with hot rice.

subSteps: Take a small gooseberry-sized ball of tamarind (about 10g).
Soak in 1/4 cup warm water for 10 minutes.
Squeeze and extract the thick pulp, discarding fibers and seeds.
VALIDATION: Tamarind should yield a thick, brown, smooth extract. Fibers and seeds should be completely separated.
Chop 3 medium ripe tomatoes into rough pieces.
Finely chop 1 medium onion (about 120g) into small pieces.
Slit 3-4 green chilies lengthwise.
Lightly crush 5-6 garlic cloves.
Finely chop 1/2 cup coriander leaves.
VALIDATION: Tomatoes should be roughly chopped, onions finely diced, chilies slit, garlic lightly crushed (not minced), coriander finely chopped.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: 1/2 cup for cooking tomatoes, 3/8 cup for consistency

subSteps: Add roughly chopped tomatoes to a heavy-bottomed pan.
Add the slit green chilies and 1/4 tsp turmeric powder.
Add 1/2 cup water.
Cook on medium flame for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally.
VALIDATION: Tomatoes should become soft and mushy, breaking down into a thick mixture. Color should deepen to a dark red-brown.
Lightly mash the cooked tomatoes using a masher or spoon, keeping some texture.
Add the tamarind extract to the tomato mixture.
Add 1 tsp salt.
Add 2 tsp jaggery.
Stir well to combine.
Simmer for 8-10 minutes on medium heat until the mixture becomes thick and glossy.
VALIDATION: Gojju should coat the back of a spoon and move slowly when tilted. It should have a glossy, thick consistency.
Heat 1.5 tbsp groundnut oil in another small pan over medium heat.
Once hot, add 1/2 tsp mustard seeds and cover immediately.
Allow seeds to splutter for 15-20 seconds until crackling stops.
Add 1/4 tsp cumin seeds, 1/2 tsp urad dal, 1 dry red chili, and 8-10 curry leaves.
Fry for 30-40 seconds until aromatic and dal turns light golden.
VALIDATION: All seeds should splutter and crackle. Urad dal should be golden. Aroma should be strong and fragrant.
Add 5-6 lightly crushed garlic cloves to the tempering.
Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the finely chopped onion.
Cook for only 2-3 minutes, stirring gently.
VALIDATION: Garlic should be fragrant but not browned. Onions should remain slightly crunchy with a bite, not fully soft.
Pour the tempering (garlic, onion, and oil) into the tomato gojju mixture.
Add 1/4 tsp roasted methi powder if using.
Stir gently to incorporate.
Simmer for 3 minutes on low heat.
Switch off the flame.
Add the finely chopped coriander leaves.
Cover and rest for 5 minutes.
VALIDATION: Gojju should be thick, glossy, and fragrant. Flavors should be balanced: tangy (tamarind), sweet (jaggery), spicy (chilies), aromatic (garlic and coriander), with crunchy onion texture.

seo.metaTitle: Tomato Gojju Recipe | South Indian Andhra Curry with Rice
seo.metaDescription: Thick and spicy tomato gojju with crunchy onions, garlic, coriander, and tamarind. Perfect curry to serve with hot steamed rice.
